issues.comment: In the Create method, when d.Storage.Create fails, the error path calls d.recordLegacyDuration instead of d.recordStorageDuration. The success path correctly calls recordStorageDuration. This is a copy-paste error that causes metrics to be recorded under the wrong histogram/counter, corrupting observability data.

In the Update method, when d.Storage.Update fails, the error path calls d.recordLegacyDuration instead of d.recordStorageDuration. The success path correctly uses recordStorageDuration. Same copy-paste error as in Create, recording legacy operation metrics instead of storage metrics.

In the Delete method, klog.NewContext is called with d.Log instead of the enriched local variable log. Every other method in the file uses ctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, log) with the enriched logger that includes 'name', 'kind', and 'method' values. Using d.Log means downstream code extracting the logger from context will get a less-informative logger missing those fields.

In the Delete method's success path, recordStorageDuration is called with `name` (the object name, e.g. 'my-object') instead of `options.Kind` (e.g. 'Pod') as the third argument. The error path correctly passes options.Kind. This creates spurious metric label values (one per object name), leading to high cardinality and incorrect metric grouping.

In the DeleteCollection method's goroutine, d.recordStorageDuration is called for the d.Legacy.DeleteCollection operation instead of d.recordLegacyDuration. All other equivalent goroutines (in Create, Delete, Update) correctly use d.recordLegacyDuration for legacy operations. This records legacy operation metrics as storage metrics.
